text: The architecture here strikes me as deliberate but impersonal, with glass barriers dividing openness from privacy. I am keenly aware of my presence; the polished surfaces and sterile lighting create a sense of transition, as if I am neither truly inside nor outside. This threshold fills me with a subtle suspense, heightening my awareness of potential and purpose—yet, at the same time, reminding me of my own transience in spaces designed for function more than comfort.
